---
title: "grdseamount"
---

# grdseamount

```julia
grdseamount(cmd0::String="", arg1=nothing; kwargs...)
```

Create synthetic seamounts (Gaussian, parabolic, polynomial, cone or disc; circular or elliptical).

(**Warning: Manual translate by Claude. Needs revision**)

Description
-----------

**grdseamount** will compute the combined topographic shape of multiple synthetic seamounts given
their individual shape parameters. We read from *table* (or pass a matrix/GMTdataset) a list of seamount
locations and sizes and can evaluate either Gaussian, parabolic, conical, polynomial or disc
shapes, which may be circular or elliptical, and optionally truncated. Various options are available
to modify the result, including an option to add in a background depth or set unmodified nodes to NaN.

The input data must contain *lon*, *lat*, *radius*, *height* for each seamount. For elliptical features
(requires **elliptical=true**), we expect *lon*, *lat*, *azimuth*, *semi-major*, *semi-minor*, *height* instead.
If flat seamount tops is specified (via **flattening**) with no value appended, then an extra column with
*flattening* is expected. For temporal evolution of topography the **time** option may be used, in which
case the data file must have two additional columns with the start and stop time of seamount construction.

Required Arguments
------------------

- *intable* : -- Input table (file name, matrix, or GMTdataset) with seamount parameters. For circular
   seamounts: *lon, lat, radius, height*. For elliptical seamounts (**elliptical=true**):
   *lon, lat, azimuth, semi-major, semi-minor, height*.

- **G** or **outgrid** : -- *outgrid="filename"*\
   Give the name of the output grid file. If **time** is set then *outgrid* must be a filename template.

{{< include ../common_opts/opt_I.qmd >}}

{{< include ../common_opts/opt_R.qmd >}}

Optional Arguments
------------------

- **A** or **mask** : -- *mask=true* **|** *mask="out/in"* **|** *mask=(out=val, in=val, scale=val)*\
   Build a mask grid only that may be used to manipulate gridded data sets to exclude or isolate
   seamount observations; append outside/inside values [1/NaN]. Height and flattening are ignored.
   Use **scale** to increase all seamount radii or semi-axes first.

- **C** or **shape** : -- *shape=:gaussian* **|** *shape=:cone* **|** *shape=:disc* **|** *shape=:parabola* **|** *shape=:polynomial*\
   Select a seamount shape function. Choose among **:cone** (c), **:disc** (d), **:gaussian** (g),
   **:polynomial** (o) and **:parabola** (p) [Default is Gaussian]. All but the disc can be
   truncated via a flattening parameter set by **flattening**. The polynomial model has amplitude
   $v(u) = (1+u)^3(1-u)^3/(1+u^3)$ where $u = r / r_0$ is the normalized radius.

- **D** or **unit** : -- *unit=:k* **|** *unit=:m* **|** *unit=:n* **|** *unit=:d*\
   Append the Cartesian unit used for horizontal distances in the input file.
   Not allowed for geographic data, which we automatically convert to km using a flat Earth assumption.

- **E** or **elliptical** : -- *elliptical=true*\
   Set elliptical data file format. We expect input records to contain
   (*lon, lat, azimuth, semi-major, semi-minor, height*) for each seamount
   [Default is Circular data format, expecting *lon, lat, radius, height*].

- **F** or **flattening** : -- *flattening=value* **|** *flattening=true*\
   Seamounts will be truncated to guyots. Append *flattening* from 0 (no flattening) up to, but not
   including, 1. If no argument is given then we expect to find the flattening in the last input column
   [no truncation]. Ignored if used with **shape=:disc**.

- **H** or **densities** : -- *densities="H/rho_l/rho_h"* **|** *densities=(H=val, rho_l=val, rho_h=val, densify=val, power=val)*\
   Set reference seamount parameters for an *ad-hoc* variable radial density function with depth.
   Give the low and high seamount densities in kg/m³ or g/cm³ and the fixed reference height *H* in meters.
   Use **densify** to change the water-pressure-driven flank density increase over the full reference
   height [0] and **power** to set the variable density profile exponent [1, linear]. The radial
   density function is:
   $$\rho(r,z) = \rho_l + \Delta \rho_f \left (\frac{H-h(r)}{H} \right ) + \Delta \rho_s \left ( \frac{h(r)-z(r)}{H} \right )^p$$

- **K** or **density_grid** : -- *density_grid="filename"*\
   Append the file name for a crossection grid with the predicted densities of the reference model.
   Uses normalized coordinates (both *x* and *y* go from 0 to 1 in increments of 0.005).

- **L** or **list_stats** : -- *list_stats=true* **|** *list_stats=hn*\
   List *area*, *volume*, and *mean height* for each seamount; no grid will be created.
   Optionally, append *hn* for a noise-floor cutoff level below which we ignore area and volume [0].

- **M** or **list** : -- *list="filename"* **|** *list=true*\
   Write the times and names of all grids created to the text file. Requires **time**.
   If no file is given then we write to standard output. The output listing is suitable
   as input to {{< gmtref grdflexure >}}.

- **N** or **normalize** : -- *normalize=value*\
   Normalize the relief grid(s) so the maximum grid height equals *value* [no normalization].

- **Q** or **build_mode** : -- *build_mode=:cumulative* **|** *build_mode=:incremental* **|** *build_mode="c/g"*\
   Set build modes. Can only be used with **time**. The default is **build_mode="c/g"**.
   - *bmode* determines how we construct the surface: **c** for cumulative volume through time
     or **i** for the incremental volume added for each time increment.
   - *fmode* determines the volume flux curve: **c** for constant volume flux or **g** for
     Gaussian volume flux [Default] between the start and stop times of each feature.

- **S** or **slide** : -- *slide=(azimuth=(...), beta=val, hc=val, heights=(...), power=val, time=(...), u0=val, volume=val)*\
   Set parameters controlling the simulation of sectoral, rotational land slides. Available modifiers:
   - **azimuth**: specifies the azimuthal sector affected by the slide [0/360]
   - **beta**: sets positive power coefficient for the normalized slide volume fraction time-curve [1]
   - **hc**: sets the flank level where the debris deposit begins [h1/2]
   - **heights**: sets the lower and upper heights of the flank source area
   - **power**: activates angular variation in the radial slide profile (power > 2)
   - **time**: sets the time span over which the slide develops (requires **time**)
   - **u0**: sets normalized radial slide shape parameter > 0 [0.2]
   - **volume**: sets desired fractional volume of the slide (in percent)

- **T** or **time** : -- *time=t0* **|** *time="t0/t1/dt"* **|** *time="t0/t1/dt+l"*\
   Specify *t0*, *t1*, and time increment (*dt*) for a sequence of calculations
   [Default is one step, with no time dependency]. Default unit is years; append **k** for kyr
   and **M** for Myr. For a logarithmic time scale, append **+l** and give the number of steps
   instead of increment. If **time** is set, then the input seamount table must have two extra
   columns for the *start* and *stop* time.

{{< include ../common_opts/opt_V.qmd >}}

- **W** or **density_output** : -- *density_output="filename"*\
   Give the name of the vertically averaged density grid file. If **time** is set then this must
   be a filename template. Requires **densities** to define the density model.

- **Z** or **level** : -- *level=value* **|** *level=NaN*\
   Set the background water depth [0]. As all seamounts have positive relief, you may
   use a larger negative *level* to place them under water. Alternatively, use *level=NaN*
   to flag unused nodes (not allowed if **build_mode=:incremental**).

{{< include ../common_opts/opt_bi.qmd >}}

{{< include ../common_opts/opt_f.qmd >}}

Slide Simulation
----------------

The simulation of land slides via **slide** is not a physical model (apart from preserving mass). Instead,
we approximate the shapes of rotational landslides via simple geometric shapes and functions.
The radial slide height is modeled as:

$$h_s(r) = h_s(u) = h_1 + (h_2 - h_1)q(u) = h_1 + (h_2 - h_1) u_0 \left (\frac{1 + u_0}{u + u_0} - 1\right ),$$

where $u = (r - r_2)/(r_1 - r_2)$ is the normalized horizontal distance of the rupture surface.

By default, the radial slide profile is fixed regardless of where in the slide sector we look. However,
angular variation can be added via the function:

$$s(\alpha) = s(\gamma) = 1 - \lvert \gamma \rvert ^p,$$

where $\gamma = 2 (\alpha - \alpha_1)/(\alpha_2 - \alpha_1) - 1$ and *p* is the power exponent.

The normalized volume rate distribution over time is simulated by:

$$\psi(t) = \psi(\tau) = \tau^\beta = \left (\frac{t - t_0}{t_1 - t_0} \right )^\beta,$$

where $\tau$ is the normalized time during a slide event and $\beta$ is set via **beta** [1].

Notes
-----

Because the Gaussian curve only drops to 1.11% of its peak amplitude at the base radius (3 sigma)
of a seamount, we actually evaluate Gaussian curves all the way to 4 sigma so that the amplitude
drops to 0.034% of peak height before we jump to zero. This prevents the otherwise very noticeable
step at the base of the seamount.

Examples
--------

To compute the shape of a circular Gaussian seamount located at 1W, 2S on a 1 arc minute grid
for a basal radius of 30 km and a height of 4500 meters:

```julia
using GMT
G = grdseamount([1 2 30 4500], region=(-1.5, -0.5, -2.5, -1.5), inc="1m", outgrid="smt.nc")
imshow(G)
```

To compute the shape of an elliptical conic seamount located at 200,400 on a 1x1 Cartesian grid
for basal semi-major axis of 35, semi-minor axis of 20, azimuth of 29, height of 3700 m, and
flattening of 0.15:

```julia
using GMT
G = grdseamount([200 400 29 35 20 3700], region=(150,250,350,450), inc=1, elliptical=true,
                flattening=0.15, shape=:cone, outgrid="ell_smt.nc")
grdview(G, shade=true, Jz="2c", view=(195,20))
```

To compute incremental loads from two elliptical, truncated Gaussian seamounts being constructed
from 3 Ma to 2 Ma and 2.8 Ma to 1.9 Ma using a constant volumetric production rate,
outputting an incremental grid every 0.1 Myr:

```julia
using GMT
# lon lat azimuth semi-major semi-minor height tstart tend
data = [0 0 -20 120 60 5000 3.0 2.0;
        50 80 -40 110 50 4000 2.8 1.9]
grdseamount(data, region="-1024/1022/-1122/924+uk", inc=2000, outgrid="smt_%3.1f_%s.nc",
            time="3M/1.9M/0.1M", build_mode="i/c", unit=:k, elliptical=true,
            flattening=0.2, shape=:gaussian, list="files.txt")
```

```{julia}
#| echo: false
#| output: asis
using GMT
GMT.doc_source_links("grdseamount")
```

Reference
---------

Smith, J. R. and Wessel, P., 2000, Isostatic consequences of giant landslides on the Hawaiian Ridge,
Pure Appl. Geophys., 157, 1097-1114.

See Also
--------

- {{< gmtref grdflexure >}}, {{< gmtref gravfft >}}
- [Alphabetical Function List](../alphabetical.html)
